name: Pat Hynes
bio: Patrick (Pat) Hynes has served as Lyons Township Assessor since 2021 and brings 30 years of property tax experience to his challenge of incumbent Fritz Kaegi. He is also a firefighter and EMT. His campaign focuses on assessment accuracy errors he attributes to Kaegi's tenure. (Source: Ballotpedia Cook County Assessor 2026)

career_history.highlight: 30 years of property tax experience; firefighter and EMT

policy_platform.position: Challenges Kaegi's assessment methodology; argues inaccuracies have driven up tax bills for homeowners.
Supports clearer communication with property owners on assessment appeals and valuation methods.
Advocates for more accurate assessments to reduce homeowner over-taxation.

social_pulse.summary: Low social media profile. Cook County assessor race challenger with township assessor backing. No major national controversies.

news_hits.headline: Cook County assessor candidates grapple over the reasons for skyrocketing property taxes
news_hits.title: Cook County assessor candidates grapple over the reasons for skyrocketing property taxes
news_hits.summary: Chicago Tribune covers the heated Kaegi-Hynes primary. Hynes argues that Kaegi's reassessment methodology shifted property tax burdens onto residential homeowners and small business owners, making the case for a fresh approach to valuations across the county's 1.8 million parcels.

news_hits.headline: Hynes challenges Kaegi for Cook County Assessor
news_hits.title: Hynes challenges Kaegi for Cook County Assessor
news_hits.summary: Southwest Regional Publishing profiles Pat Hynes, Lyons Township assessor and nephew of former Cook County Assessor Thomas Hynes, who is challenging two-term incumbent Fritz Kaegi in the March 17 Democratic primary.

news_hits.headline: Property tax collection rates drop in Chicago neighborhoods with big bill hikes
news_hits.title: Property tax collection rates drop in Chicago neighborhoods with big bill hikes
news_hits.summary: Chicago Tribune reports that several community leaders in neighborhoods hit hardest by assessment increases have endorsed Hynes over Kaegi, citing property tax affordability concerns.

news_hits.title: Five Illinois congressional seats, Senate seat open in historic election year
news_hits.summary: WBEZ comprehensive March 17 primary preview covers all key races including Cook County Board President (Preckwinkle vs. Reilly), Cook County Assessor (Kaegi vs. Hynes), U.S. Senate, governor, and comptroller.

news_hits.title: What elections are coming up in Illinois? Here's what's ahead in the 2026 primary
news_hits.summary: NBC Chicago overview of key 2026 primary races, covering Preckwinkle vs. Reilly for Cook County Board President and Kaegi vs. Hynes for Assessor among the notable local contests.
